Tests the behavior of JavaScript functions after deleting `Function.prototype.toString`.

# Purpose
This code snippet performs two main actions. First, it deletes the `toString` method from the `Function.prototype`, which affects how functions are converted to strings. Second, it uses an `assert` statement to check if the result of adding `1` to the string representation of `Math.cos` equals the string `"[object Function]1"`. This code is likely used to test or demonstrate the behavior of JavaScript functions when their `toString` method is removed, and how this affects operations involving function objects and type coercion.
